subject: Chemistry
chapter: Classification of Elements and Periodicity in Properties

questions.q: The elements with atomic numbers 3, 11, 19 and 37 belong to the same group. What are these groups?
questions.answer: Group 13, Group 17, Group 19 and Group 1 respectively.

questions.q: What is meant by a 'lanthanoid contraction'?
questions.answer: The lanthanoid contraction is the phenomenon due to which the atomic and ionic radii of the lanthanoids are less than what is expected from the general trend of the periodic table.

questions.q: Which of the following elements does not exhibit the duet and octet rule?
How many elements are present in the 4th period of the periodic table?
What is the reason for the decrease in the atomic radii of elements down a group?
questions.answer: The reason for the decrease in the atomic radii of elements down a group is due to the increase in the effective nuclear charge, which pulls the electrons closer to the nucleus.

questions.q: Which of the following is not a characteristic of noble gases?
What is the name of the series of elements in which the atomic number increases by 1 when we move from one element to the next?
What is the electronic configuration of the element with atomic number 54?
Which of the following elements is a metalloid?
What is the reason for the increasing order of electronegativity of the elements in a group?
questions.answer: The reason for the increasing order of electronegativity of elements in a group is due to the increase in the effective nuclear charge, which pulls the electrons closer to the nucleus.
